module.title: SAP TM Organizational Structure & Master Data

topic.title: SAP TM Organizational Model & Organizational Units

learningObjectives.title: Understand TM organizational model
learningObjectives.description: Explain how SAP TM organizational units define transportation responsibilities.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate planning organization and group
learningObjectives.description: Clarify scope and purpose of planning organizations versus planning groups.

learningObjectives.title: Identify planner responsibilities
learningObjectives.description: Recognize key planning, optimization and execution-monitoring duties.

sections.content: The SAP TM Organizational Model defines the organizational hierarchy responsible for transportation planning, execution, monitoring and freight settlement. It controls how transportation activities are managed, assigned and reported within an organization.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Enterprise Structure defines where business happens, whereas the TM Organizational Model defines who performs transportation activities.
Planning Organizations represent the business units responsible for transportation planning. They determine which planners can create, modify and optimize transportation plans.
Planning Groups categorize transportation planners based on geography, business unit or transportation responsibility. This improves workload distribution and reporting.
Transportation Planners create Freight Orders, optimize Freight Units, assign carriers, monitor transportation execution and resolve transportation exceptions.

ABC Logistics has separate planning teams for North, South, East and West regions. Each Planning Organization manages transportation within its region, while Planning Groups divide work among transportation planners responsible for different customers.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A global logistics provider created separate Planning Organizations for Asia, Europe and North America. Each region had dedicated Planning Groups responsible for domestic and international transportation planning.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Interviewers frequently ask the difference between Enterprise Structure and Organizational Model. Enterprise Structure defines business entities, whereas the Organizational Model defines transportation responsibilities.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Planning Organization with Company Code. Company Code belongs to Enterprise Structure, while Planning Organization exists only within SAP TM.

items: Enterprise Structure and Organizational Model are different concepts.
